source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Synchronous slash command (no LLM call)
notes: Spec asserts: state stays ready during /cost and /help (both genuinely local — see parser.go isLocalCommandContent, which filters their <local-command> wrappers from the transcript). The recipe issues a warm-up turn FIRST so the UUID transcript materializes (claude only writes the JSONL file once it has a real LLM turn to record; without the warm-up, resolve_transcript hangs forever). After warm_turn_end returns to ready, the slash_window phase pins ready with duration_at_least_ms:4000 and invariants forbidding state_transition to working/waiting — those are what would fire if /cost or /help (incorrectly) drove a model call.

text: Warm-up prompt drives ready → working (real LLM turn; materializes the transcript so subsequent driver steps can resolve_transcript)
Warm-up turn completes back to ready
Spec: state stays ready throughout /cost and /help — no working transition driven by either synchronous slash command
